document.heading: Amendments to Title 2, Chapter 4 of the Chickasaw Nation Code (Salaries of Officers)

para.text: The basis for authority of this Act is Article VI, Section 1 of the Constitution, wherein the Legislature is granted the legislative authority of the Chickasaw Nation. In Article VII, Section 4, the Legislature is granted the power and authority to enact rules and regulations pertaining to the Chickasaw Nation. In Article VII, Section 11, "The Tribal Legislature shall have the power to fix and prescribe salaries and allowances for all elected or appointed officials and employees of the Nation. The Tribal Legislature shall review said salaries and allowances every fourth year and shall increase or decrease as necessary."

The Chickasaw Tribal Legislature, with the adoption of this Act, hereby establishes the procedures whereby annual salaries for all elected officials of the Chickasaw Nation shall be reviewed and revised in accordance with Article VII, Section 11 of the Constitution.

The Executive, Legislative, and Judicial Departments shall be recognized as coequal and independent in the exercise of their respective powers and duties. All elected officials serving in any of these departments shall accrue credit for each year of service rendered, which shall be duly recognized for the purposes of calculating salary and benefits. Such recognition of service shall be applied uniformly and equitably, without regard to the specific department in which the service was performed.
